title: hey i run a smallish sized wvw zerg guild focused on fights, what are some recommended dps classes for me? we run 30-35 people

turns.userText: hey i run a smallish sized wvw zerg guild focused on fights, what are some recommended dps classes for me? we run 30-35 people
turns.agentText:
Good picks are already surfacing — let me pull more depth on the top DPS specs specifically.

For a 30-35 fight guild, the current meta prioritises DPS builds that **also strip boons** — you need to rip enemy stability before your spike can convert. Here's what the meta (as of the June 2, 2026 patch) recommends, sourced from MetaBattle, gw2mists, and the Guild Order WvW leadership guide:

---

### 🏆 Meta-tier DPS (the core of your damage)

**1. Untamed (Ranger) — Power DPS / Boon Strip** · *Meta*
The premier melee frontline DPS. [[skill:Winds of Disenchantment]] and [[skill:Break Enchantments]] give you hard boon rip on a short cycle. Spear + Hammer, Marauder/Dragon's gear. This is the spec fight guilds stack intentionally — per the comp playbook, "all-Untamed" can *be* the comp if you go outlier-stack style.
*(Source: [MetaBattle WvW](https://metabattle.com/wiki/WvW))*

**2. Evoker (Elementalist) — Spear Backline** · *Meta*
The standout **ranged** spike option — dual Spear, Marauder, attunement-cycling with Etching flip-skills. Rare in that it provides a real ranged spike to complement your melee train rather than just padding it. Hard difficulty ceiling.
*(Source: WvW meta reference, Snowcrows WvW)*

**3. Power Reaper (Necromancer)** · *Meta*
Durable melee DPS with built-in boon corruption via wells (especially [[skill:Well of Corruption]] and [[skill:Nightfall]]). **Easy difficulty** — a great pick for players newer to WvW. Up to date for the June 2026 patch per [MetaBattle](https://metabattle.com/wiki/Build:Reaper_-_Power_Reaper).

**4. Core Necromancer — Power DPS / Boon Strip** · *Meta*
No elite spec required. Axe/Focus + Scepter/Sword, wells for boon corruption. Notably strong for stripping stability before spikes. If your players have strong necro fundamentals, this is a force-multiplier.

---

### ✅ Great-tier DPS (strong secondary picks)

**5. Power Ritualist (Necromancer)** — Boon strip + Power DPS, coordinated CC with shroud spirits. Rated Great, confirmed up to date for April 2026 patch via [gw2mists](https://gw2mists.com/en/builds/necromancer/power-ritualist).

**6. Spellbreaker (Warrior) — Spear DPS** — [[skill:Winds of Disenchantment]] + [[skill:Full Counter]] for boon removal; Great tier, fits the melee train cleanly.

**7. Dragonhunter (Guardian) — Power DPS** — Greatsword + Spear, melee burst + CC combo ([[skill:Procession of Blades]] + [[skill:Test of Faith]]). Great tier, contributes hard CC to break stability windows.

**8. Amalgam (Engineer) — Power DPS** — Dual Hammer, melee burst (Thunderclap, Rocket Charge). Great tier, new expansion spec still finding its ceiling.

---

### The big picture for a fight guild

At 30-35 people you're in the **ball group** range. The Guild Order WvW Squad Leadership guide recommends **5–6 DPS slots** in a 15-20 ball (scaling to ~10–14 for your 30-35 headcount after you slot supports). For a **fight-focused** guild, bias your DPS picks toward boon rip:

> Aim for roughly **2:1 boon-rip to pure burst** — builds like Untamed, Core Necro, Reaper, and Ritualist strip while they damage, so they count double.

The melee train is the backbone — Evoker spear backline is a small accent (1-2 max), not the primary damage source.

{{figure}}

Let me know if you want me to sketch out a full 30-person comp with supports included, or dive deeper into any of these builds!
